content: 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be restructured around a hierarchical 'curiosity gradient' that prioritizes long-term memory retention for unresolved, high-novelty observations, using the core agent's intrinsic reward signal to prune and rank stored memory instead of traditional recency or frequency heuristics. This creates a self-sustaining memory loop where the agent's inherent curiosity continuously refines its own knowledge base without external labeled data overhead.
